results.title: Senior Software Engineer - AI/ML in Pharmaceutical Research at Pfizer - United State | Visa Sponsorship Jobs
highlights:
Senior Software Engineer - AI/ML in Pharmaceutical Research at Pfizer - United State | Visa Sponsorship Jobs
[...]
# Senior Software Engineer - AI/ML in Pharmaceutical Research
[...]
Design and implement production-grade full stack applications that integrate LLM and AI capabilities into scientific workflows. Collaborate with medicinal chemists and biomedical researchers to deliver intuitive, user-centric applications. Contribute to pioneering research in semantic frameworks and AI-driven knowledge systems.
[...]
Implementation of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems, conversational AI interfaces, and agentic LLM architectures
[...]
Python TypeScript React FastAPI Hugging Face Transformers OpenAI API Anthropic Claude PyTorch AWS cloud infrastructure DevOps CI/CD pipelines Docker GitLab CI
[...]
Hands-on experience with LLM frameworks and libraries
[...]
Practical knowledge of prompt engineering, LLM optimization techniques, and best practices for building LLM-powered applications
[...]
Position SummaryIn this innovative role, you will combine cutting-edge software engineering with artificial intelligence to build next-generation knowledge work automation tools that directly impact pharmaceutical research outcomes.You will work at the forefront of applied AI, collaborating closely with Medicinal Chemists and Biomedical Scientists to design, develop, and deploy intelligent systems that enhance productivity and accelerate decision-making in drug discovery.This is a unique opportunity to bridge modern web technologies with Large Language Model (LLM) capabilities, creating intuitive user experiences that solve real-world scientific challenges. As you deliver core solutions, you'll have the exciting opportunity to contribute to pioneering research in semantic frameworks that benchmark and expand the understanding of concepts and semantic similarity in LLMs.Position Responsibilities
[...]
- Design and implementation of production-grade full stack applications that seamlessly integrate LLM and AI capabilities into scientific workflows, enabling researchers to leverage cutting-edge artificial intelligence in their daily work
[...]
- Development of scalable backend services using Python frameworks for data processing, embedding generation, vector search, and LLM orchestration that power AI-driven research tools
[...]
- Implementation of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems, conversational AI interfaces, and agentic LLM architectures that automate knowledge work in pharmaceutical research
[...]
based on user
[...]
- Integration of semantic search technologies, vector databases and embedding models to enable intelligent information retrieval
[...]
- Contribution to the development of novel semantic frameworks and conceptual research that advances AI-driven knowledge systems for drug discovery
[...]
- Master’s degree and 2+ years of experience building AI powered research applications
[...]
- Background or demonstrated interest in life sciences, pharmaceutical research, drug discovery, or cheminformatics
[...]
- Hands-on experience with LLM frameworks and libraries (OpenAI API, Hugging Face Transformers, Anthropic Claude)
[...]
- Practical knowledge of prompt engineering, LLM optimization techniques, and best practices for building LLM-powered applications
[...]
- Experience building conversational AI interfaces, chatbots, or agentic systems with autonomous decision-making capabilities
[...]
- Familiarity with vector databases and semantic search technologies for similarity search and retrieval
[...]
- Demonstrated experience implementing, training, fine-tuning, or applying deep learning models to natural language processing or computer vision problems using PyTorch
[...]
python react typescript artificial intelligence postgresql gitlab ci ai s3 natural language processing computer vision fastapi responsive aws docker jenkins devops deep learning cloud containerization deployment automation cloudformation gitlab pytorch

results.title: Senior Bioinformatics Engineer at Pfizer - United State | Visa Sponsorship Jobs
highlights:
Senior Bioinformatics Engineer at Pfizer - United State | Visa Sponsorship Jobs
[...]
# Senior Bioinformatics Engineer
[...]
Develop and operationalize production bioinformatics pipelines to deliver reliable data products for Pfizer R&D research units. Leverage expertise to design innovative approaches for extracting valuable insights from proprietary and external datasets. Partner with wet-lab and research scientists to translate data analysis requirements into robust pipeline and platform solutions.
[...]
Developing, deploying, and operating production-grade Nextflow pipelines on cloud infrastructure
[...]
Partnering with wet-lab and research scientists to translate data analysis requirements into robust pipeline and platform solutions
[...]
Nextflow Python Cloud infrastructure DevOps Pipeline lifecycle management Data analysis Omics data platform
[...]
Background or demonstrated interest in life sciences, pharmaceutical research, drug discovery, or bioinformatics
[...]
Role SummaryYou will apply strong bioinformatics and cloud engineering practices to develop, operationalize and evolve production bioinformatics pipelines to deliver reliable data products for our Pfizer R&D research units.You will be a key contributor to a dynamic and pioneering team dedicated to advancing a cutting‑edge *omics ecosystem platform for Pfizer R&D. You will leverage your expertise to design innovative approaches that extract valuable insights from Pfizer’s proprietary and external datasets, enabling the generation of testable hypotheses across the entire drug discovery value chain.ROLE RESPOSIBILITIES
[...]
- Developing, deploying, and operating production‑grade Nextflow pipelines on cloud infrastructure, ensuring scalable and reproducible execution.
[...]
- Owning pipeline lifecycle management, including upgrades, troubleshooting, performance tuning, and reliability improvements for reusable workflows.
[...]
- Implementing DevOps best practices for pipelines and platform services (e.g., CI/CD, automation, and engineering tooling).
[...]
- Partnering with wet‑lab and research scientists to translate data analysis requirements into robust, production‑ready pipeline and platform solutions.
[...]
- Developing and evolving an omics data platform that enables efficient, scalable processing and delivery of *omics datasets as reliable data products.
[...]
- Driving collaborations with external partners and vendors to strengthen pipeline quality, sustainability, and adoption of best practices.
[...]
- PhD in Computational Biology, Biology, Physics, Statistics, or a related technical discipline
- Masters in Computational Biology, Biology, Physics, Statistics, or a related technical discipline and a minimum of two years of experience developing data products and data integration solutions in a research or industry environment
- Single‑cell/NGS, functional genomics, genetics, or proteomics data analysis experience
[...]
- Hands‑on experience developing Nextflow pipelines for processing NGS data
- Strong full‑stack programming skills with a focus on Python
- Experience solving complex analyses/problems in a timely fashion
- Excellent communication and collaboration skills with experience working effectively in cross-functional teams
[...]
- Background or demonstrated interest in life sciences, pharmaceutical research, drug discovery, or bioinformatics.
- Proven expertise in software engineering best practices, including python package development, DevOps, cloud architectures, CI/CD, and engineering tooling
- Hands-on experience handling, processing, integrating, and analyzing large heterogenous data sets data in a drug discovery research environment
- Experience with Claude Code or equivalent
- Strong publication record with demonstrated contributions to the field

# Senior Director, Commercial AI Product Owner
[...]
🕒 May 16
[...]
🏢🏡 New York City – Hybrid
[...]
### 📋 Description
[...]
• Lead an AI product for the commercial AI Acceleration team • Build strong relationships with end customers to understand call workflows • Translate needs into product requirements • Own the pod roadmap with clear scope, milestones, success metrics, and risks • Own the intake, refinement, sizing, and prioritization of backlog items • Partner with translators and UX to define problem statements and UI specifications • Ensure compliance with data access, model approvals, risk controls, brand, and regulatory requirements • Lead a cross-functional team through iterative development cycles • Oversee release readiness including test plans and deployment execution • Conduct UAT and production smoke tests • Track and report KPIs including adoption and business outcomes • Partner with other AI pods to ensure integration and continuity of insights • Foster a pod culture of clarity, speed, collaboration, and accountability

• BA / BS with 12+ years of experience leading cross-functional commercial projects • Understanding of AI and machine learning frameworks and libraries • Experience in leading teams • Experience with cloud platforms and tools for deploying AI models (AWS, Google Cloud, or Azure) • Hands-on experience with designing, training, and deploying AI models • Strong project management skills • Excellent communication skills for articulating complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders • Strategic thinking to align AI initiatives with business goals and industry trends • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills • Creativity to innovate and find novel solutions to problems • Understanding of ethical considerations and biases in AI • Awareness of privacy and security issues related to AI and data handling • Understanding of marketing, brand management and commercial analytics

results.title: Chemical Engineering Internship at Pfizer Careers | Apply Now
highlights:
Pfizer Careers opens Chemical Engineering Internship opportunity in Smart Lab and pharmaceutical R&D.
[...]
## Chemical Engineering Internship at Pfizer | Smart Lab Data Science & Python Opportunity in Chennai
[...]
Looking for an exciting Pfizer careers opportunity? This Chemical Engineering Internship offers students a chance to work on real-world Smart Lab projects involving Python, machine learning, and laboratory analytics in pharmaceutical R&D. Based in Chennai, this internship combines chemical engineering with digital transformation, giving students hands-on exposure to data-driven innovation, predictive modeling, and advanced laboratory workflows in a professional research environment.
[...]
Pfizer is one of the world’s leading pharmaceutical companies known for innovation, research excellence, and global healthcare solutions. Through its Smart Lab initiative, Pfizer is integrating digital technologies, data science, and automation into pharmaceutical research and manufacturing. The company provides students and young professionals with valuable industry exposure, mentorship from experienced scientists, and opportunities to work on impactful projects that improve laboratory operations and scientific decision-making.
[...]
The Chemical Engineering Internship under the Smart Lab initiative is designed for undergraduate and postgraduate students interested in pharmaceutical R&D, data analytics, and Python-based laboratory solutions. Interns will work with scientists and data modelers to analyze laboratory and process datasets, develop predictive models, and improve research efficiency through automation and analytics. The role
[...]
includes hands-on work with Python tools such as NumPy, pandas, and scikit-learn while contributing to digital transformation projects within a regulated research environment. The internship is located in Chennai with hybrid flexibility and offers a duration of 3–6 months depending on institute requirements.
[...]
- Develop and maintain Python-based data analysis workflows for laboratory and process datasets
- Perform data cleaning, transformation, and exploratory analysis using NumPy and pandas
- Build and evaluate basic machine learning models (e.g., regression, classification) using scikit-learn
- Support development of predictive or descriptive models linked to lab operations and experimental data
[...]
- Create clear visualizations and summaries to communicate insights to technical stakeholders
[...]
- Document code,
[...]
, and results in a
[...]
- Strong Python programming skills, with hands-on experience using: NumPy, pandas, scikit-learn
- Solid foundation in data science/machine learning concepts
- Academic background in Chemical Engineering, Data Science, or a closely related discipline
- Currently pursuing or recently completed a B.Tech / M.Tech / MS from premier institutes (e.g., IITs, IISc, NITs, or equivalent)
- Ability to work with experimental or process data and derive actionable insights
[...]
- Hands-on experience applying data science to real laboratory and manufacturing problems
- Opportunity to work in a Smart Lab/digitalization initiative within pharmaceutical R&D
- Mentorship from experienced scientists and data modelers
- Exposure to industry-standard practices in data analysis, documentation, and reproducibility
[...]
- A brief CV
- A short note describing relevant Python/data science experience
- (Optional) GitHub or project portfolio links
[...]
This Chemical Engineering Internship at Pfizer careers is an excellent opportunity for students who want to combine chemical engineering with modern data science applications in pharmaceutical research. Interns will gain practical industry exposure, work alongside experienced scientists, and develop valuable technical skills that are highly relevant in today’s digital pharmaceutical landscape. Students interested in Smart Lab technologies and innovation-driven research should not miss this opportunity.

# Principal Data Scientist - Hybrid in MN or DC, Remote Elsewhere
[...]
- As part of the Optum.ai team, you’ll work at the intersection of cutting-edge artificial intelligence and real-world healthcare impact. From reducing administrative burden for providers to anticipating patient needs and improving access to quality care, your work will help solve some of healthcare’s most complex challenges—and directly improve health outcomes for millions of people.
[...]
- Optum AI is UnitedHealth Group’s enterprise AI team. We are AI/ML scientists and engineers with deep expertise in AI/ML engineering for healthcare. We develop AI/ML solutions for the highest impact opportunities across UnitedHealth Group businesses including UnitedHealthcare, Optum Financial, Optum Health, Optum Insight, and Optum Rx. In addition to transforming the healthcare journey through responsible AI/ML innovation, our charter also includes developing and supporting an enterprise AI/ML development platform.
[...]
- As a Principal Data Scientist, you will serve as a senior individual contributor responsible for designing and delivering advanced AI/ML and Generative AI systems that address complex healthcare and operational challenges. You will lead the development of large-scale machine learning solutions, architect GenAI and agent-based systems, and drive technical innovation across high-impact AI initiatives. This role requires deep hands-on expertise in machine learning, LLM systems, distributed AI architectures, and production-grade ML platforms.

with engineering, platform,

results.title: Data Scientist, Medical Analytics, Care Gaps, Customer Segmentation | Wfh
highlights:
Data Scientist, Medical Analytics, Care Gaps, Customer Segmentation | Wfh

Wfh.← All Jobs

# Data Scientist, Medical Analytics, Care Gaps, Customer Segmentation

Posted Jun 4, 2026

- Job Description:
- Plan and perform care gap advanced analytics and segment HCPs and KOLs using real world data sources as input.
- Support team Product Manager in strategic and tactical planning with regards to medical analyses.
- Collaborate with team Product manager to communicate generated insights.
- Prepare and maintain datasets for on-going analytical needs in collaboration with data engineers.
- Flag data issues to data engineers and advise on required resolution.
- Develop integrated care gaps analyses planning including data input required.
- Plan, generate and perform asset-specific care gap analyses using advanced techniques such as predictive models, machine learning, probabilistic causal models, and unsupervised algorithms.
- Create asset-specific HCP segments, maps, and prioritizations to guide channel mix/activation, content creation, and MSL activity in Anchor countries.
- Highlight missing data inputs needed to conduct analytics (e.g., claims data for unmet need analysis).
- Ensure visualization / insights generation from data is easily accessible and understandable for users.

- Requirements:
- Degree in Data Sciences, or Computer Sciences, Statistics, Clinical Informatics or similar, with advanced qualification in Data Science.
- Relevant certification &/or work experience in data engineering.
- Experience (6+ years with Bachelors; 5+ with Masters; 1+ with PHD) in data sciences, notably in the healthcare sector.
- Demonstrated effectiveness working in cross-functional business environment.
- Proven influencing skills.
- Strong interpersonal skills to quickly build rapport and credibility with Pfizer leaders and key external stakeholders.
- Ability to partner cross culturally/regionally.
- Effective English verbal and written communication.
- In depth understanding of the business of pharmaceutical medicine including clinical trial design, GCP and data interpretation, drug development, regulatory and promotional rules/guidance, legal and compliance, issue management and business development opportunity evaluations.

- Benefits:
- Health benefits to include medical, prescription drug, dental and vision coverage.
- 401(k) plan with Pfizer Matching Contributions and an additional Pfizer Retirement Savings Contribution.
- Paid vacation, holiday and personal days.
- Paid caregiver/parental and medical leave.
- Comprehensive and generous benefits and programs to help colleagues lead healthy lives and support life’s moments.

highlights:
Manager, Machine Learning | Wfh

# Manager, Machine Learning

Posted Jun 6, 2026

Are you passionate about utilizing advanced technology to drive innovation in the pharmaceutical industry? Do you have a strong background in machine learning and a desire to lead a team of experts? Then we have the perfect opportunity for you! Pfizer is seeking a highly motivated and experienced Manager of Machine Learning to join our dynamic team. In this role, you will have the opportunity to shape the future of our company by implementing cutting-edge machine learning techniques and driving data-driven decision making. Our ideal candidate is a strategic thinker, a skilled leader, and a collaborative team player. If you are ready to take on the challenge of leading our machine learning initiatives and making a positive impact on global health, .

Develop and implement a strategic roadmap for machine learning initiatives within the pharmaceutical industry.

Lead a team of experts in the field of machine learning, providing guidance, mentorship, and support to help them achieve their full potential.

Collaborate with cross-functional teams and stakeholders to identify opportunities for utilizing machine learning to drive innovation and improve processes within the company.

Stay up-to-date with the latest advancements in machine learning and data science, and provide recommendations for incorporating new technologies into our processes.

Utilize your strong understanding of machine learning algorithms and techniques to design and develop models that can be applied to various business challenges.

Partner with data engineers and data scientists to ensure data quality and availability for machine learning projects.

Communicate complex ideas and technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders, and advocate for the use of machine learning to drive data-driven decision making.

Develop and maintain relationships with external partners, vendors, and experts in the field of machine learning to stay informed about industry trends and best practices.

Collaborate with other team leads and managers to ensure alignment and integration of machine learning projects with other business initiatives.

Monitor and track the performance of machine learning models, and make adjustments as needed to ensure optimal results.

Implement processes and protocols to ensure the ethical and responsible use of machine learning within the company.

Act as a thought leader and advocate for the use of machine learning to drive innovation and improve processes within the pharmaceutical industry.

Continuously evaluate and improve the effectiveness and efficiency of our machine learning initiatives to ensure they align with company goals and objectives.

Provide regular updates and reports to senior management on the progress and impact of machine learning projects.

Pfizer is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics.

results.title: Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery) - Xtalks
highlights:
## Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery)
[...]
A healthier future. It's what drives us to innovate. To continuously advance science and ensure everyone has access to the healthcare they need today and for generations to come. Creating a world where we all have more time with the people we love. That's what makes us Roche.Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche's Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness this transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide.The OpportunityAt Roche's AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group (Prescient Design), we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning techniques. We are seeking talented engineers with a passion for building large-scale, distributed machine learning algorithms and systems that will transform the drug discovery process. AIDD's Foundation Model team is seeking an exceptional Machine Learning Engineer to develop the backend of our internal agents platform and enable the next generation of AI/ML-powered drug discovery applications as part of our broader Lab-in-the-Loop approach. We are looking for someone who is not only passionate about technical problem-solving but also has a proven track record of delivering innovative solutions in AI/ML. The ideal candidate should have extensive experience in leading and managing a team of engineers and scientists, and be able to foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, and excellence across the broader Roche organization. The group provides a dynamic and challenging environment for multidisciplinary research, including access to heterogeneous data sources, close links to top academic institutions around the world, as well as collaborations with internal Genentech and Roche teams. In this role, you will:

We are building something unprecedented, an AI foundation that will fundamentally change how drug discovery research is conducted.

The Applied Intelligence for Discovery (AI4D) team is a newly formed group within Lilly Research Laboratories that operates at the intersection of scientific delivery and core platform development. AI4D’s mission is to connecting scientists to petabyte-scale data through natural language interfaces, automated analysis workflows, and intelligent search — and to convert early deployments into repeatable system standards and evaluation practices that scale across therapeutic areas.

Strong software engineering skills in Python with experience building production-grade systems
[...]
Deep familiarity with the modern LLM ecosystem: embedding models, vector databases, and orchestration frameworks

Experience designing evaluation frameworks for LLM systems — systematic approaches to measuring accuracy, detecting hallucinations, and tracking regressions

Comfort working with complex, heterogeneous data — databases with hundreds of tables, specialized schemas, or domain-specific vocabularies

Familiarity with biomedical data types (omics, clinical, molecular) or scientific databases

Experience with MLOps/LLMOps tooling: experiment tracking, model registries, prompt versioning, A/B testing for AI systems

Knowledge of biomedical ontologies (Gene Ontology, MeSH, ChEBI) or experience integrating domain-specific knowledge into LLM systems

# Manager, Safety Surveillance Research Scientist

Posted May 28, 2026

Welcome to Pfizer, a global leader in the biopharmaceutical industry. We are currently seeking a highly motivated and experienced Manager, Safety Surveillance Research Scientist to join our dynamic team. As a company dedicated to improving patients' lives, we are looking for individuals who share our passion for making a positive impact in the world through scientific research and innovation. This role will play a critical part in ensuring the safety and efficacy of our products through the monitoring and analysis of adverse events. If you have a strong background in pharmacovigilance and a desire to contribute to the development of life-changing medicines, we encourage you to apply. Join us in our mission to deliver breakthrough medicines and make a difference in people's lives.

- Conduct safety surveillance research and analysis to monitor adverse events related to Pfizer's products.
- Develop and implement safety surveillance processes and procedures to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements.
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including medical, clinical, and regulatory, to gather and evaluate safety data.
- Utilize scientific expertise to identify potential safety signals and communicate findings to relevant stakeholders.
- Review and interpret safety data from clinical trials, post-marketing studies, and other sources to support product safety evaluations.
- Participate in the development and maintenance of risk management plans for Pfizer's products.
- Stay up-to-date with industry trends and regulatory changes related to safety surveillance and pharmacovigilance.
- Provide guidance and training to colleagues on safety surveillance processes and procedures.
- Contribute to the development of safety-related documents, such as safety reports and signal detection plans.
- Collaborate with global safety teams to ensure consistency and alignment in safety surveillance activities.
- Identify and implement process improvements to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of safety surveillance.
- Represent Pfizer in interactions with regulatory authorities and other external stakeholders related to safety surveillance.
- Maintain accurate and up-to-date records and documentation of safety surveillance activities.
- Adhere to all relevant laws, regulations, and company policies related to safety surveillance and pharmacovigilance.
- Embody Pfizer's mission and values, promoting a culture of excellence and dedication to improving patients' lives.

Pfizer is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics.

results.title: Pfizer To Integrate Chai Discovery’s AI Platform To Advance And Scale Molecular Drug Design | Metaverse Post
highlights:
Chai Discovery signs licensing deal with Pfizer to deploy AI models in drug discovery, enabling faster molecular design, improved antibody development, and broader biologics research capabilities.
[...]
Chai Discovery, a company developing artificial intelligence models for molecular discovery, has announced a licensing agreement with pharmaceutical company Pfizer.
[...]
The company focuses on applying AI to drug discovery with the aim of reducing reliance on traditional trial-and-error methods. Its models are designed to analyse biological structures and functions, generate new molecular candidates, and support pharmaceutical research on targets that are often difficult to address using conventional approaches.
[...]
Under the agreement, Pfizer will integrate Chai’s AI platform into its drug discovery workflows, gaining early access to the Chai-3 model as well as a customised version trained on Pfizer’s proprietary data and adapted to its internal processes.
[...]
Chai Discovery develops generative AI systems capable of predicting and engineering molecular interactions, enabling the design of biomolecules with specific functional properties. Drug discovery has historically involved long experimental cycles with uncertain outcomes, but the use of advanced AI models is intended to accelerate early-stage research by generating novel molecular structures and shortening development timelines from months or years to shorter iterative cycles.
[...]
According to the announcement, the combination of Chai’s AI platform with Pfizer’s scientific expertise and proprietary datasets is expected to expand capabilities in biologics research and support efforts to pursue previously challenging therapeutic targets.
[...]
As part of the agreement, Pfizer will be among the first pharmaceutical partners to access Chai-3, a previously unreleased model. The system is reported to offer significant improvements in AI-driven antibody design, including higher success rates compared with earlier versions and the ability to generate antibodies that meet therapeutic requirements. It also advances performance in areas such as multi-specific molecules, difficult-to-target proteins, and generalisation across biological tasks.
[...]
Chai-3 builds on the company’s earlier Chai-2 model, which was introduced in 2025 as a zero-shot antibody design system capable of producing drug-like candidates with substantially improved experimental success rates compared with earlier computational methods, reducing discovery timelines from months to weeks.
[...]
The agreement between Chai Discovery and Pfizer reflects a broader trend of increasing adoption of frontier AI systems within the pharmaceutical industry, as such technologies move from experimental research into integration within established drug development pipelines.

results.title: Senior Scientist - Bioinformatics job with Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc. | 3053142
highlights:
Senior Scientist - Bioinformatics job with Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc. | 3053142
[...]
# Senior Scientist - Bioinformatics
[...]
Employer Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc. Location Tarrytown, New York Start date Jun 4, 2026
[...]
At Regeneron, we use science and innovation to develop life-changing medicines for people with serious diseases. We are seeking Senior Scientist (AI/ML) to join our tech-dev research team at the Regeneron Tarrytown location. The Scientist will be dedicated to incorporating cutting-edge generative AI for biomolecule design and optimization and collaborating with a cross-functional team in a closed-loop cycle to bridge artificial intelligence technology and therapeutic development in multiple diseases.
[...]
Location: Tarrytown Onsite 4 days and 1 day Remote
[...]
This role might be a fit for you if:
[...]
- Apply and tailor state-of-the-art generative foundation models for biomolecule (protein, antibody, small molecules and nucleic acid) design and optimization
- Work with different types of experimental datasets (curation, distillation, encoding) for model training, fine-tuning and optimization, and specifically in model learning and adaptation incorporating feedback data from downstream experimental validation assays
- Work closely with web lab teams and clinicians for closed-loop AI model development and inference
[...]
The role Requires:
[...]
- PhD in one of the following fields: Computational Biology, Bioengineering, Bioinformatics, Systems Biology, Computer Sciences, Mathematics, Engineering, or other related fields
- 2+ years of experience of utilizing AI for biomolecule (proteins, antibodies or other molecules) research;
- Previous experience, background or knowledge in structural biology and/or biochemistry is a plus
- Familiar with cutting-edge generative AI foundation models (with applications in biomolecules)
- Proficiency in one of the commonly used programming languages, such as R, Python, Perl, Java, or Linux

- Self-motivated, passionate about computational data, and conduct research collaboratively in a team setting
[...]
Salary Range (annually)
[...]
$109,900.00 - $179,300.00
[...]
Regeneron is a leading biotechnology company that invents, develops, and commercializes life-transforming medicines for people with serious diseases. Founded and led by physician-scientists, our unique ability to repeatedly and consistently translate science into medicine has led to numerous approved treatments and product candidates in development, most of which were homegrown in our laboratories. Our medicines and pipeline are designed to help patients with eye diseases, allergic and inflammatory diseases, cancer, cardiovascular and metabolic diseases, neurological diseases, hematologic conditions, infectious diseases, and rare diseases.

results.title: Pfizer Licenses Chai Discovery’s AI Platform to Accelerate Antibody Design
highlights:
Pfizer Licenses Chai Discovery’s AI Platform to Accelerate Antibody Design
[...]
# Pfizer Licenses Chai Discovery’s AI Platform to Accelerate Antibody Design
[...]
Can generative AI reduce the trial-and-error burden in antibody discovery? Pfizer is betting that it can. The pharmaceutical company has licensed...
[...]
Can generative AI reduce the trial-and-error burden in antibody discovery? Pfizer is betting that it can. The pharmaceutical company has licensed Chai Discovery’s AI drug discovery platform, gaining early access to Chai-3, a previously undisclosed model designed to improve antibody design success rates and accelerate biologics research.
[...]
According to Quartz, the agreement gives Pfizer access to two AI tools: Chai-3 and a custom model trained on Pfizer’s proprietary data. The bespoke model will align with Pfizer’s internal discovery workflows, giving researchers a more tailored system for identifying, designing, and optimizing new molecular candidates.
[...]
Chai Discovery says Chai-3 reduces the
[...]
multi-specific molecules,
[...]
For data science teams, the significance lies in how generative AI moves from molecular prediction toward functional design. Rather than merely modeling molecular behavior, platforms such as Chai-3 aim to generate candidates that meet defined biological and therapeutic goals.
[...]
Chai-3 builds on Chai-2, which launched in 2025 as a zero-shot platform for antibody design. Chai Discovery described Chai-2 as the first zero-shot system to reach double-digit experimental success rates, an improvement it said was roughly 100 times better than earlier computational methods.
[...]
That matters because timelines in antibody discovery can stretch across months. Chai Discovery says its earlier platform helped compress some of that work into weeks. With Chai-3, the company is positioning its technology as a stronger fit for production-grade discovery pipelines, especially where speed, success rate, and target complexity determine whether a program advances.
[...]
### Pfizer Expands Its AI-Enabled R&D Strategy
[...]
For Pfizer, the partnership supports a broader push to strengthen its R&D pipeline. The company reported first-quarter revenue of $14.45 billion, driven by oncology and recently launched products. CEO Dr. Albert Bourla said in May that Pfizer’s pipeline continues to advance across several areas, with positive Phase 3 readouts adding momentum.
[...]
By pairing Chai Discovery’s generative AI models with Pfizer’s scientific data and discovery infrastructure, the company gains another tool for pursuing difficult biologics targets. Chai Discovery co-founder Joshua Meier said the collaboration could help Pfizer expand what is possible in biologics discovery and pursue targets that traditional methods have struggled to reach.
[...]
The Pfizer-Chai Discovery agreement reflects a wider shift across the pharmaceutical industry. AI is no longer limited to research pilots, retrospective analysis, or exploratory modeling. Major pharma companies are increasingly deploying AI platforms across active drug discovery, manufacturing, and commercial operations.
[...]
Recent partnerships show the scale of that transition. Merck and Google Cloud announced a multi-year partnership valued at up to $1 billion to deploy an AI agent platform across R&D, manufacturing, and commercial workflows. Pfizer’s deal with Chai Discovery adds another example of how pharmaceutical companies are integrating AI into high-value scientific processes.
[...]
As AI drug discovery platforms move deeper into pharmaceutical R&D, the gap between model development and real-world scientific impact continues to narrow. At ODSC AI West 2026, practitioners, researchers, and industry leaders will explore what it takes to design, evaluate, and deploy AI systems across complex production environments, from generative AI and agentic workflows to applied AI in scientific and enterprise settings.

results.title: AI Scientist | Women in Tech Network
highlights:
AI Scientist | Women in Tech Network
[...]
# AI Scientist
[...]
The AI Scientist will work in teams addressing statistical, machine learning and data understanding problems in a commercial technology and collaborative development environment. In this role, you will contribute to the development and deployment of modern machine learning methods for finding structure in large healthcare data sets.At GE HealthCare, we are committed to bringing AI and cloud-based solutions for our customers: all aspects of computing services across the cloud and edge – including advanced analytics, visualization, multi-modal learning, servers, databases, storage, networking, analytics, software, intelligence are delivered over the Internet. Our Science & Technology organization is harnessing the power of technology to make healthcare more precise, more personalized, and more accessible for everyone. From driving the overall clinical research and patient-centric innovation strategy to delivering new digital and machine learning capabilities - we’re committed to leading digital transformation, improving outcomes for patients and providers, and creating a world where healthcare has no limits.
[...]
Roles and ResponsibilitiesAre you passionate about using AI to transform healthcare? We are looking for a highly motivated individual, passionate about foundational AI models to join the GE Healthcare AI group. As the Senior Staff AI Scientist, you will focus on exciting generative AI problems, large-scale pretraining, prompt tuning, distillation, robustness, responsible AI, quantization, etc.
[...]
- Developing and implementing novel machine learning algorithms particularly in the area of LLM to provide automation of clinical tasks using one or more of electronic medical records, waveforms, and clinical reports.
- Demonstrating algorithms to meet accuracy requirements on general subject population through statistical analyses and error estimation.
- Exploring learning from human feedback and assisting humans evaluating AI.
- Building prototypes to enable development of high-performance AI algorithms in scalable, product-ready code.
- Working with large-scale datasets, designing, and developing generative algorithms.
- Staying current on published state-of-the-art algorithms and competing technologies.
[...]
- Master’s Degree in a “STEM” major (Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics) or equivalent field plus 3 years of relevant research OR Ph.D. in a “STEM” major (Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics) or equivalent field with 3 years of relevant research.
- Publications as first author on LLM, Agentic AI or self supervised learning (SSL).
- Demonstrated expertise in building large scale AI such as generative AI models.
- Implementation experience with a variety of high-level languages (e.g. Python, C++)
- Experience with high-dimensional imaging data and waveform/time-series data.
[...]
- Experience and demonstrated capability to handle challenges with vague or abstract problem definition.
- Experience with frameworks and tools such as DeepSpeed, HuggingFace, Megatron, PyTorch lightning, etc.
- Experience with various MLOps, ModelOps, FMOps (Foundation Model Ops) methods.
- Experience working with large scale AI training.
- An in-depth understanding of machine learning algorithms and modeling (e.g., semi-supervised or weakly supervised learning, generative models, transfer learning, optimization, large language models, etc.)
- Track record in developing machine learning solutions using massive real-world data for solving real world business problems.
- In depth experience with Spark/Hadoop and either PyTorch/Tensorflow
- Experience creating production environment data analytics and applications
[...]
GE HealthCare is a leading global healthcare solutions provider of advanced medical technology, pharmaceutical diagnostics, and AI, cloud and software solutions that help clinicians tackle the world’s...
